name: Baku (Azerbaijan)

colors.symbolism: the three golden flames of the city arms

description: A light-blue field charged centrally with the coat of arms of Baku — three golden flames over a stylised wave on a shield, surrounded by the city's emblems.
history: Baku, capital of Azerbaijan and one of its eleven cities of republican significance, adopted its modern flag together with a restored coat of arms after independence in 1991. The three flames recall both the city's name (from Persian for "city of God / wind") and the natural petroleum fires of the Absheron Peninsula that have made Baku famous since antiquity.

features.symbolism: three flames over a wave — Baku as the historic city of fire on the Caspian Sea
